title: tabernanthe elliptica
intro: Tabernanthe elliptica, also known as the Tabernanthe or Madagascar periwinkle, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. It is rich in alkaloids such as vincamine, which has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing various health benefits including improved blood circulation and reduced oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
